SEARCH
Package io.stimulustech.filequeue

Class FileQueueItem

java.lang.Object
io.stimulustech.filequeue.FileQueueItem
public abstract class FileQueueItem extends Object
Extend this abstract class to include the properties of a queue item. For example, an ID that refers to a queued element. Fields are serialized via Jackson; the class does not need to implement Serializable.

Each instance is only ever accessed by one thread at a time: the framework serialises items to/from JSON across every thread hand-off, so no concurrent access to the same object occurs and atomic types are unnecessary.

Author:
Jamie Band, Valentin Popov

    int
    getTryCount()
    Returns the number of delivery attempts made so far.

    Returns the timestamp of the last delivery attempt, or null if the item has not yet been attempted.

    Increments the try count by one.

  • Constructor Details

    • FileQueueItem

      protected FileQueueItem()
      Default constructor for subclasses and Jackson deserialisation.

  • Method Details

    • getTryDate

      public Instant getTryDate()
      Returns the timestamp of the last delivery attempt, or null if the item has not yet been attempted. Instant is immutable so no defensive copy is needed.
      Returns:
      last try timestamp, or null

    • setTryDate

      public void setTryDate(Instant instant)
      Sets the timestamp of the last delivery attempt.
      Parameters:
      instant - timestamp to record, or null to clear

    • getTryCount

      public int getTryCount()
      Returns the number of delivery attempts made so far.
      Returns:
      current try count

    • setTryCount

      public void setTryCount(int tryCount)
      Overrides the try count.
      Parameters:
      tryCount - new count; must be >= 0
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if tryCount is negative

    • incTryCount

      public void incTryCount()
      Increments the try count by one.

    • toString

      public abstract String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
